1	import {
2	  AllowFromListSchema,
3	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
4	  DmPolicySchema,
5	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
6	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives";
7	import { buildSecretInputSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input";
8	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
9	
10	/**
11	 * Validates https:// URLs only (no javascript:, data:, file:, etc.)
12	 */
13	const safeUrlSchema = z
14	  .string()
15	  .url()
16	  .refine(
17	    (url) => {
18	      try {
19	        const parsed = new URL(url);
20	        return parsed.protocol === "https:";
21	      } catch {
22	        return false;
23	      }
24	    },
25	    { message: "URL must use https:// protocol" },
26	  );
27	
28	/**
29	 * NIP-01 profile metadata schema
30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),
66	
67	  /** Optional default account id for routing/account selection. */
68	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
69	
70	  /** Whether this channel is enabled */
71	  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
72	
73	  /** Markdown formatting overrides (tables). */
74	  markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
75	
76	  /** Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format */
77	  privateKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional(),
78	
79	  /** WebSocket relay URLs to connect to */
80	  relays: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
81	
82	  /** DM access policy: pairing, allowlist, open, or disabled */
83	  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
84	
85	  /** Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex format) */
86	  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
87	
88	  /** Profile metadata (NIP-01 kind:0 content) */
89	  profile: NostrProfileSchema.optional(),
90	});
91	
92	export type NostrConfig = z.infer<typeof NostrConfigSchema>;
93	
94	/**
95	 * JSON Schema for Control UI (converted from Zod)
96	 */
97	export const nostrChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(NostrConfigSchema);
98

1	import {
2	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
3	  BlockStreamingCoalesceSchema,
4	  DmConfigSchema,
5	  DmPolicySchema,
6	  GroupPolicySchema,
7	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
8	  ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
9	  ToolPolicySchema,
10	  requireOpenAllowFrom,
11	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema";
12	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
13	import { ircChannelConfigUiHints } from "./config-ui-hints.js";
14	
15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
80	  });
81	});
82	
83	export const IrcConfigSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.extend({
84	  accounts: z.record(z.string(), IrcAccountSchema.optional()).optional(),
85	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
86	}).superRefine((value, ctx) => {
87	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
88	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
89	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
90	    ctx,
91	    path: ["allowFrom"],
92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
93	  });
94	});
95	
96	export const IrcChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(IrcConfigSchema, {
97	  uiHints: ircChannelConfigUiHints,
98	});

Perfect! I have now read all the source files in the Nostr plugin. Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary of all the files:

## Complete Nostr Channel Plugin - All Source Files

I've successfully read all 27+ files from the Nostr plugin at `/extensions/nostr/`. Here's a comprehensive breakdown:

### Root Configuration Files

1. **openclaw.plugin.json** - Plugin metadata with ID "nostr", no additional config schema
2. **package.json** - Version 2026.4.1-beta.1, depends on nostr-tools v2.23.3
3. **index.ts** - Main entry point, exports nostrPlugin and HTTP handler registration
4. **setup-entry.ts** - Setup plugin entry point
5. **api.ts** - Re-exports from runtime-api.js (runtime barrel)
6. **runtime-api.ts** - Re-exports from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr"
7. **setup-api.ts** - Exports nostrSetupAdapter and nostrSetupWizard
8. **test-api.ts** - Exports nostrPlugin for testing

### Core Source Files (`src/`)

#### 1. **channel.ts** (17.3 KB)
Main plugin implementation. Contains:
- `nostrPlugin` - Complete chat channel plugin definition
- Account startup/gateway management with NIP-04 DM handling
- Inbound DM authorization and routing
- Outbound DM sending via active bus handles
- Pairing challenge notifications
- Metrics snapshot caching
- Profile publishing and state tracking functions
- Helper functions for metrics and bus management

#### 2. **config-schema.ts** (2.7 KB)
Zod schemas for configuration:
- `NostrProfileSchema` - NIP-01 profile metadata (name, displayName, about, picture, banner, website, nip05, lud16)
- `NostrConfigSchema` - Full channel config with account name, private key, relays, DM policy, allowlist, profile
- Safe URL validation (https:// only)
- JSON schema conversion for Control UI

#### 3. **types.ts** (3.4 KB)
Account resolution and type definitions:
- `ResolvedNostrAccount` interface with public/private keys, relays, config
- Account listing and default resolution from config
- Private key handling (hex or nsec bech32 format)
- Public key derivation from private keys

#### 4. **runtime.ts** (317 bytes)
Runtime store initialization using `createPluginRuntimeStore`

#### 5. **default-relays.ts** (73 bytes)
Default relay URLs: `wss://relay.damus.io`, `wss://nos.lol`

#### 6. **nostr-bus.ts** (26.4 KB) - LARGE FILE
Core Nostr protocol implementation:
- `NostrBusOptions` and `NostrBusHandle` interfaces
- Circuit breaker pattern for relay health management
- Relay health tracker with success rate and latency metrics
- DM subscription (kind:4 events) with NIP-04 decryption
- Rate limiting (per-sender and global) with fixed window algorithm
- Seen event tracker with LRU eviction
- State persistence with debouncing
- Profile publishing with monotonic timestamp guarantee
- Key validation (hex and nsec format)
- Encrypted DM sending with relay health-based routing

#### 7. **session-route.ts** (646 bytes)
Outbound session routing - strips channel prefix and builds session routes

#### 8. **setup-surface.ts** (8.8 KB)
Setup wizard and adapter:
- `nostrSetupAdapter` for config validation and patching
- `nostrSetupWizard` with interactive credential prompts
- Relay URL and allowlist parsing
- DM policy configuration UI
- Environment variable support (NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY)

#### 9. **metrics.ts** (12 KB)
Comprehensive metrics system:
- Event metrics (received, processed, duplicate, rejected reasons)
- Relay metrics (connections, errors, circuit breaker states)
- Rate limit and decrypt tracking
- Memory stats (seen tracker size, limiter entries)
- `MetricEvent` callbacks and `MetricsSnapshot` snapshots
- Per-relay stats aggregation

#### 10. **nostr-profile.ts** (8 KB)
Profile management (NIP-01 kind:0 events):
- Profile creation and signing
- Profile content format conversion (camelCase ↔ snake_case)
- Profile publishing to relays with per-relay timeouts
- Profile validation and XSS-safe display sanitization
- Monotonic timestamp enforcement for replaceable events

#### 11. **nostr-profile-http.ts** (17.9 KB)
HTTP request handler for profile operations:
- GET /api/channels/nostr/:accountId/profile - Fetch current state
- PUT /api/channels/nostr/:accountId/profile - Update and publish
- POST /api/channels/nostr/:accountId/profile/import - Import from relays
- Rate limiting (5 requests/minute per account)
- Mutex to prevent concurrent publishes
- SSRF protection (https:// URLs, private IP blocking)
- CSRF guards (loopback-only mutations, origin/referer checks)
- Request body size limits and timeouts

#### 12. **nostr-profile-import.ts** (7.1 KB)
Profile fetching from relays:
- `importProfileFromRelays()` - Query all relays for kind:0 events
- Event signature verification and deduplication
- Profile content parsing and validation
- URL sanitization to prevent SSRF
- Profile merging strategy (local fields override imported)
- Relay timeout handling and parallel queries

#### 13. **nostr-state-store.ts** (7.2 KB)
Persistent state management:
- `NostrBusState` v2 schema with recent event IDs for restart recovery
- `NostrProfileState` tracking last publish time and per-relay results
- State file organization by account ID
- Version migration from v1 to v2
- Atomic file writes with temp files and rename
- Secure file permissions (0o600)

#### 14. **seen-tracker.ts** (6.2 KB)
LRU cache with TTL for duplicate detection:
- Doubly-linked list for LRU ordering
- TTL expiration (default 1 hour)
- Prune interval for cleanup
- Methods: has, add, peek, delete, clear, size, stop, seed

#### 15. **test-fixtures.ts** (1.4 KB)
Test helper constants and builders:
- Test private/public keys (hex and nsec formats)
- Test relay URLs
- `createConfiguredNostrCfg()` and `buildResolvedNostrAccount()`

### Test Files

- **channel.test.ts** (12.6 KB)
- **channel.inbound.test.ts** (5.5 KB)
- **channel.outbound.test.ts** (2.6 KB)
- **nostr-bus.test.ts** (6.2 KB)
- **nostr-bus.inbound.test.ts** (12.7 KB)
- **nostr-bus.fuzz.test.ts** (18.3 KB)
- **nostr-bus.integration.test.ts** (15.8 KB)
- **nostr-profile.test.ts** (12.5 KB)
- **nostr-profile.fuzz.test.ts** (15.3 KB)
- **nostr-profile-http.test.ts** (15.4 KB)
- **nostr-profile-import.test.ts** (3.8 KB)
- **nostr-state-store.test.ts** (7.1 KB)
- **test/setup.ts** (175 bytes)

### Documentation

- **README.md** (4.2 KB)
- **CHANGELOG.md** (2.1 KB)

---

## Architecture Summary

**Design Pattern**: Modular plugin architecture with:
- Central `NostrBus` managing NIP-04 DMs and relay connections
- Circuit breaker + health tracking for relay resilience
- Pre-crypto and post-crypto authorization layers
- Persistent state with debounced writes
- HTTP API for profile management with security guards
- Comprehensive metrics collection

**Key Features**:
- NIP-04 encrypted direct messages
- DM access control (pairing, allowlist, open, disabled)
- Profile publishing with best-effort relay distribution
- SSRF protection and loopback-only mutations
- Rate limiting with fixed-window algorithm
- Seen event tracking with LRU + TTL
- Full message lifecycle tracking and metrics

All files are now available for use as a template. The codebase is comprehensive and production-grade with extensive test coverage.

## Plan: PulseBoard Channel Plugin

**19 new files** under `extensions/pulseboard/` — no existing files touched.

### What gets created

**Root files (5):**
- `openclaw.plugin.json` — manifest declaring id `pulseboard`, channel `pulseboard`
- `package.json` — `@openclaw/pulseboard`, no external deps (native `fetch`)
- `index.ts` — `defineChannelPluginEntry` wiring `pulseboardPlugin` + `setPulseboardRuntime`
- `setup-entry.ts` — `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseboardPlugin)`
- `api.ts` — public barrel re-exporting accounts + setup surface

**Source files (14):**
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/types.ts` | `ResolvedPulseboardAccount`, `PulseboardMessage`, `PulseboardProbe`, `CoreConfig` |
| `src/config-schema.ts` | Zod schema → `PulseboardChannelConfigSchema` via `buildChannelConfigSchema` |
| `src/runtime.ts` | `createPluginRuntimeStore` → `getPulseboardRuntime` / `setPulseboardRuntime` |
| `src/runtime-api.ts` | Barrel re-exporting SDK utilities from individual `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths (no dedicated barrel exists for pulseboard) |
| `src/accounts.ts` | `resolvePulseboardAccount`, `listPulseboardAccountIds`, `resolveDefaultPulseboardAccountId` |
| `src/normalize.ts` | `#board` → `board:name`, `@user` → `dm:userid`; `isBoardTarget`, `extractBoardName`, etc. |
| `src/client.ts` | Fictional HTTP client stub — `pollPulseboardUpdates`, `sendPulseboardDm`, `sendPulseboardBoard`, `probePulseboardApi` |
| `src/send.ts` | `sendMessagePulseboard(to, text, opts)` — routes to DM or board via client |
| `src/inbound.ts` | `handlePulseboardInbound` — DM policy check (disabled/open/allowlist/pairing), dispatch |
| `src/monitor.ts` | Polling loop: 2s interval, `AbortSignal`-driven teardown, routes to `handlePulseboardInbound` |
| `src/probe.ts` | `probePulseboard` — hits `/api/v1/me`, returns `PulseboardProbe` |
| `src/setup-surface.ts` | `pulseboardSetupAdapter` + `pulseboardSetupWizard` (server URL, token, userId, username prompts) |
| `src/channel.ts` | `pulseboardPlugin` via `createChatChannelPlugin` — id, meta, capabilities (`direct`+`group`), config adapter, DM security resolver, gateway (`startAccount` → monitor), pairing, outbound (4000-char limit, markdown chunker) |
| `src/channel.test.ts` | Vitest tests for account resolution, target normalization, plugin shape |

### Key design decisions
- **Target format**: `#boardname` / `board:boardname` for boards; `@userid` / `dm:userid` for DMs
- **Text chunk limit**: 4000 chars, markdown mode, mirrors Telegram
- **Delivery mode**: `"direct"` (synchronous send), mirrors IRC
- **DM policy**: pairing / allowlist / open / disabled — full policy suite like IRC
- **Monitor**: HTTP long-poll every 2s, AbortSignal teardown — no real WebSocket since PulseBoard is fictional
- **No external npm deps**: uses Node 22 native `fetch`

### Flagged uncertainties (resolve by reading referenced files)
- Exact `runtime-api.ts` subpaths for `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`, `getChatChannelMeta` — read `extensions/irc/src/runtime-api.ts`
- `buildChannelConfigSchema` import path — read `extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts`
- `ChannelSetupWizard` field shapes — read `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts`
- `createAccountStatusSink` method names in monitor — read `extensions/irc/src/monitor.ts`
- Zod version (`"zod"` vs `"zod/v4"`) — check existing plugins

### Verification
```bash
pnpm test -- extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.test.ts
pnpm check
openclaw channels status --probe   # after adding config
```

---

## Plan: PulseBoard Channel Plugin

**19 new files** under `extensions/pulseboard/` — no existing files modified.

### Architecture
PulseBoard supports `direct` (DMs) and `group` (boards) chat types with a 4000-char text limit. It uses a polling monitor (HTTP `GET /api/v1/updates`) for inbound messages and REST endpoints for outbound. The fictional client uses native `fetch` with Bearer auth — no external npm dependencies needed.

### Key design decisions
| Concern | Decision |
|---|---|
| Target format | `#boardname` → `board:boardname`, `@userid` / bare `userid` → `dm:userid` |
| Text limit | 4000 chars, markdown chunker mode |
| Delivery | `"direct"` mode, mirrors IRC |
| DM policy | Full pairing / allowlist / open / disabled suite |
| Monitor | HTTP poll every 2s, `AbortSignal`-driven teardown |
| Multi-account | Yes, via `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter` (mirrors IRC) |

### Files
- **5 root files**: manifest, package.json, index.ts, setup-entry.ts, api.ts  
- **14 source files**: types, config-schema (Zod), runtime store, runtime-api barrel, accounts, normalize, HTTP client stub, send, inbound, monitor, probe, setup-surface, channel (main plugin), tests

### Flagged uncertainties
The plan notes 6 specific import paths that must be confirmed by reading `extensions/irc/src/runtime-api.ts` and related IRC files before writing the final code (e.g., exact subpath for `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`, `buildChannelConfigSchema`).

### Verification
```bash
pnpm test -- extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.test.ts
pnpm check
```

| `src/client.ts` | Fictional HTTP client stub — `pollPulseboardUpdates`, `sendPulseboardDm`, `sendPulseboardBoard`, `probePulseboardApi` |
| `src/send.ts` | `sendMessagePulseboard(to, text, opts)` — routes to DM or board via client |
| `src/inbound.ts` | `handlePulseboardInbound` — DM policy check (disabled/open/allowlist/pairing), dispatch |
| `src/monitor.ts` | Polling loop: 2s interval, `AbortSignal`-driven teardown, routes to `handlePulseboardInbound` |
| `src/probe.ts` | `probePulseboard` — hits `/api/v1/me`, returns `PulseboardProbe` |
| `src/setup-surface.ts` | `pulseboardSetupAdapter` + `pulseboardSetupWizard` (server URL, token, userId, username prompts) |
| `src/channel.ts` | `pulseboardPlugin` via `createChatChannelPlugin` — id, meta, capabilities (`direct`+`group`), config adapter, DM security resolver, gateway (`startAccount` → monitor), pairing, outbound (4000-char limit, markdown chunker) |
| `src/channel.test.ts` | Vitest tests for account resolution, target normalization, plugin shape |

### Key design decisions
- **Target format**: `#boardname` / `board:boardname` for boards; `@userid` / `dm:userid` for DMs
- **Text chunk limit**: 4000 chars, markdown mode, mirrors Telegram
- **Delivery mode**: `"direct"` (synchronous send), mirrors IRC
- **DM policy**: pairing / allowlist / open / disabled — full policy suite like IRC
- **Monitor**: HTTP long-poll every 2s, AbortSignal teardown — no real WebSocket since PulseBoard is fictional
- **No external npm deps**: uses Node 22 native `fetch`

### Flagged uncertainties (resolve by reading referenced files)
- Exact `runtime-api.ts` subpaths for `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`, `getChatChannelMeta` — read `extensions/irc/src/runtime-api.ts`
- `buildChannelConfigSchema` import path — read `extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts`
- `ChannelSetupWizard` field shapes — read `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts`
- `createAccountStatusSink` method names in monitor — read `extensions/irc/src/monitor.ts`
- Zod version (`"zod"` vs `"zod/v4"`) — check existing plugins

### Verification
```bash
pnpm test -- extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.test.ts
pnpm check
openclaw channels status --probe   # after adding config
```

ChatType: "direct",
    ConversationLabel: params.conversationLabel,
    SenderId: params.senderId,

    ...params.extraContext,
  });

  await recordInboundSessionAndDispatchReply({
    cfg: params.cfg,
    channel: params.channel,
    accountId: route.accountId ?? params.accountId,
    agentId: route.agentId,
    routeSessionKey: route.sessionKey,
    storePath,
    ctxPayload,
    recordInboundSession: params.runtime.channel.session.recordInboundSession,

      params.runtime.channel.reply.dispatchReplyWithBufferedBlockDispatcher,
    deliver: params.deliver,
    onRecordError: params.onRecordError,
    onDispatchError: params.onDispatchError,
  });

  return {
    route,
    storePath,
    ctxPayload,
  };
}

content.text:
All imports verified. The PulseBoard plugin implementation is complete. Here's a summary of all 20 files created under `extensions/pulseboard/`:

**Root files (5):**
- `openclaw.plugin.json` — plugin manifest
- `package.json` — package metadata with `openclaw` block  
- `index.ts` — main entry with `defineChannelPluginEntry`
- `setup-entry.ts` — setup-phase entry with `defineSetupPluginEntry`
- `api.ts` — public barrel re-exporting from accounts + setup-surface

**Source files (15):**
- `src/types.ts` — all TypeScript types (`PulseboardAccountConfig`, `ResolvedPulseboardAccount`, `PulseboardMessage`, `PulseboardProbe`, `CoreConfig`)
- `src/config-schema.ts` — Zod schemas + channel config schema for the Control UI
- `src/runtime.ts` — `getPulseboardRuntime`/`setPulseboardRuntime` via `createPluginRuntimeStore`
- `src/runtime-api.ts` — private barrel re-exporting from individual `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths (no dedicated pulseboard barrel exists)
- `src/accounts.ts` — multi-account resolution via `createAccountListHelpers` + `resolveMergedAccountConfig`
- `src/normalize.ts` — target normalization (`#board` → `board:name`, `@user` → `dm:userid`)
- `src/chunk.ts` — local `chunkTextForOutbound` implementation (not in public SDK entrypoints)
- `src/client.ts` — fictional PulseBoard HTTP API client using native `fetch`
- `src/send.ts` — `sendMessagePulseboard` routing DMs and board posts
- `src/inbound.ts` — inbound handler using `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime` with `extraContext: { ChatType: "group" }` override for board messages; DM policy gate with pairing store integration
- `src/monitor.ts` — polling loop (2s interval) with abort-signal teardown
- `src/probe.ts` — API health probe via `/api/v1/me`
- `src/setup-surface.ts` — `pulseboardSetupAdapter` + `pulseboardSetupWizard` with `createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, DM policy section, allowlist section
- `src/channel.ts` — main `pulseboardPlugin` via `createChatChannelPlugin` with full base/pairing/security/outbound config
- `src/channel.test.ts` — Vitest tests covering accounts, normalize, chunk, and plugin shape
